description: KDBX4 with a single inner-header attachment marked protected (flag byte 0x01). kdbxweb writes a `ProtectedValue`-wrapped binary as plaintext on disk plus the protected flag — the same convention KeePassXC uses. Earlier revisions of the unlock pipeline mistakenly XOR-ed protected inner-header binaries with the inner-stream keystream, corrupting attachment payloads and desynchronising the cipher for every following protected `<Value>` in XML; this fixture pins the regression. The entry also carries a non-default protected custom field (`TOTP Seed`), which decrypts cleanly only after the cipher fix.
